summary: DHH argues that human customer service is becoming a luxury good as AI replaces commodity-level support. While acknowledging AI's promise, he contends that current AI customer service often traps users in frustrating loops when they just need a human. He distinguishes between low-end support that nobody will miss and skilled human agents who tailor interactions to both the problem and the customer's temperament. Basecamp's thesis is that treating customer service as a career produces agents who deliver this luxury experience.
key_points: Current AI customer service frequently traps users in frustrating loops, making human access feel like a premium feature
Humans need more than correct answers — they need understanding, reassurance, and genuine connection
AI will replace commodity support that was already done poorly by disengaged workers at cost-obsessed companies
Skilled human agents read temperament as well as problems, delivering bespoke interactions that AI cannot replicate
Treating customer service as a career rather than a cost center is Basecamp's competitive differentiator
money_quotes: I can't tell you how often I've been stuck in some god-forsaken AI loop or phone tree WHEN ALL I WANT IS A HUMAN.
In our excitement about the technical promise, I think we're forgetting that humans need more than correct answers.
Customer service at its best also offers understanding and reassurance. It offers a human connection.
Nobody is going to cry a tear for losing that.
A customer service agent who's good at their job knows how to tailor the interaction not just to your problem, but to your temperament.
They can spot an angry-but-actually-just-frustrated fit a thousand miles away.
By treating customer service as a career, we'll end up with the kind of agents that embody this luxury, and our customers will feel the difference.
key_insight: As AI commoditizes basic customer support, companies that invest in skilled human agents will turn customer service into a luxury differentiator rather than a cost center.
